title: Step 4 – Creating a Project Breakdown Structure and Resource Allocation Plan
summary: Guides you through building a Project Breakdown Structure (PBS) to break the work into clear packages, estimate effort, map dependencies, and assign team members and timelines for effective resource management.
content: Break the project into manageable work packages using a Project Breakdown Structure (PBS). Map each work package to the team members responsible, indicating effort estimates and dates. This visual plan highlights dependencies, resource allocation, and accountability, enabling the team to see how deliverables are assembled.
can_answer: How do I create a Project Breakdown Structure?
How can I assign resources and estimate effort in a PID?
examples: Phase 1: Research (2 weeks) – Lead: Jane Doe; Phase 2: Wireframes (3 weeks) – Lead: John Smith.
warnings: Over‑detailed breakdowns can become difficult to maintain.
